task: Type-3 DFIG external LVRT trip logic drawing and integration plan

gui_recon_summary.draft_status: absorbed into formal plan and deleted from the working tree

VIBR1_2.location_summary: Type-3 branch 22 kV collector-side measurement point, upstream of BRK_DFIG
VIBR1_2.confirmed_fact: measurement location and offline waveform availability are confirmed

BRK_DFIG.location_summary: breaker between the VIBR1_2 measurement point and XFMR_DFIG_22_33
BRK_DFIG.confirmed_fact: existing command path and state monitor are visible near the breaker

project_lvrt_rule.Vs_ge_0p90_without_trip: end continuous low-voltage event and reset timer state
project_lvrt_rule.trip_latch: latched for the current simulation; no auto-reclose

timer_method: minimum-voltage latch within continuous low-voltage event

state_machine.minimum_voltage_latch: Vs_min_latched is updated to the minimum Vs observed during the continuous low-voltage event
state_machine.latch_behavior: once trip_latch=1, it remains set for the simulation

candidate_voltage_interface.status: measurement location confirmed; control-source wiring and pu scaling unconfirmed
candidate_voltage_interface.future_integration_requirement: sample from the corresponding physical measurement/control output in parallel, not from the PGB output label or .out channel
required_confirmations: actual real-time control output port of the measurement module
pu base and scaling consistency with VIBR1_2
need for filtering or signal validity check

breaker_command_interface.status: existing command path and state monitor observed; control input polarity and command semantics unconfirmed
required_confirmations: existing BRK_DFIG command source and default closed command value
whether the command must be pulse, level, delayed, or otherwise shaped

command_combiner_status: TBD; do not assume polarity or logic gate before GUI confirmation

monitoring_signals.control_use: monitoring only; do not feed these monitoring outputs back into the LVRT protection control

planned_validation_cases.expected: trip_latch should not set; BRK_DFIG should not open; Type-3 branch should recover
trip_latch should set during the continuous low-voltage event; actual BRK_DFIG opening depends on later command combiner implementation
check whether post-clear voltage below 0.90 pu remains part of the continuous low-voltage event

r0p10_d1p25.note: offline state-machine prediction only; actual PSCAD trip time can differ after wiring

0p75_s_case: Type-3 branch remains connected and recovers
1p25_s_case: trip_latch sets near the offline reference time after control-source scaling is confirmed
if breaker connection has been implemented later, DFIG_BRK_STATE changes consistently with opening
PIBR1_2 and QIBR1_2 change consistently with branch cut after actual opening
1p00_s_case: used to verify continuous-event timing interpretation and post-clear below-0.90 behavior

unresolved_items: VIBR1_2 real-time control-source branch point and pu scaling
BRK_DFIG command polarity and port name
whether trip_latch monitoring channels should be added before breaker connection
future_manual_integration_sequence: Confirm the physical measurement module/control output corresponding to VIBR1_2.
Confirm voltage pu base and scaling.
Confirm BRK_DFIG control port, open-command polarity, and existing command source.
Confirm a safe TBD command combiner method.
Build the external LVRT state machine without connecting it to BRK_DFIG; output trip_latch monitor first.
Validate 0.10 ohm / 0.75 s no false trip.
Only after command semantics are confirmed, connect the combiner output to BRK_DFIG.
Rerun 0.75 s, 1.00 s, and 1.25 s cases.
non_goals: no PSCAD implementation in this task
no BRK_DFIG connection in this task
no fault or cascade logic changes
no claim that the full paper model has been reproduced
